methods.description: Describes the origin of samples (marine sand, macrophytes, benthic, plankton net) and the process for isolating single cells to obtain monoclonal cultures. Details specific growth conditions (medium, salinity, temperature, light cycle, light intensity) for different clones.

steps.description: The detailed sampling information of the newly described taxa in this manuscript is summarized in Table 1. Clones SZCZCH1247, HK315, and HK391 were isolated from marine sand. Clone HK325 was isolated from macrophytes, whereas HK445 and HK446 from an unspecified benthic environment. The remaining clones were collected by plankton net from the shallow marine environment.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect environmental samples from marine habitats

steps.description: Single cells were isolated from wild samples to obtain monoclonal cultures.
steps.searchable_summary: Isolate single cells from environmental samples

steps.description: Serratifera varisterna was grown in enriched seawater at a salinity of 35 f/2 medium (Guillard 1975) at 18°C under a 16:8 light:dark cycle, illuminated with 50 lmol photons Á m À2 Á s À1 of white light. SZCZCH1247 was grown in the same condition as Serratifera varisterna, but at a salinity of 30. Clone HK385 was grown in a salinity of 40 f/2 medium, at 27°C under a 12:12 light:dark cycle, illuminated with 21 lmol photons Á m À2 Á s À1 of white light. With the same light conditions, clone HK315 was grown at a salinity of 35 f/2 medium, at 27°C. The remaining clones were grown at a salinity of 35 PSU f/2 medium at 20°C-24°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Culture isolated cells under specific conditions

methods.description: Describes the use of Light Microscopy (LM) and Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) to examine samples. Notes that specific protocols from external publications were followed.

steps.description: Microscopic work within this study has been shared between University of Szczecin and University of Texas at Austin. LM and SEM protocols at Austin and Szczecin follow Ashworth (2013) and Li et al. (2015), respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform microscopic examination using LM and SEM following established protocols

methods.description: Describes the process of extracting genomic DNA from cell cultures and amplifying specific ribosomal RNA (SSU) and chloroplast genes (rbcL, psbC) using Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR). Includes details on DNA extraction kit, primers, reaction setup, thermal cycling conditions, product visualization, purification, and sequencing.

steps.description: For clones SZCZCH168 and SZCZC1247, several milliliters of cell suspension from an exponentially growing culture were harvested to extract the genomic DNA...
steps.searchable_summary: Harvest cell culture for DNA extraction

steps.description: ...using the Genomic DNA NucleoSpin â Plant II Kit (Qiagen, Hilden, Germany) according to the manufacturer's instructions.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract genomic DNA using a commercial kit
inputs: Genomic DNA NucleoSpin Plant II Kit

steps.description: The primers for PCR amplification and sequencing of the small subunit (SSU) of ribosomal RNA, and two chloroplast genes (rbcL and psbC) were described in Alverson and Kolnick (2005) and Alverson et al. (2007). The volume of each PCR was 25 lL: 2 lL (20 ng) purified DNA template; 2.5 lL 109 Dream Taq buffer (includes 20 mM MgCl 2 ); 1 lL Ultrapure dNTPs Set (5 mM each dATP, dCTP, dGTP, dTTP); 0.5 lL each primer (10 lM); 0.15 lL Dream Taq DNA polymerase (5 U Á lL À1 ); and ddH 2 O to a final volume of 25 lL.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare PCR reaction mix for SSU, rbcL, and psbC amplification

steps.description: PCR conditions for SSU were as follows: 94°C for 2 min, 35 cycles of (94°C for 15 s, 55°C for 15 s, 72°C for 1 min and 35 s), and final extension at 72°C for 7 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform PCR amplification for SSU gene

steps.description: PCR conditions for psbC and rbcL were the same as with SSU but with 53°C for annealing temperature and 1 min and 15 s for extension time.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform PCR amplification for psbC and rbcL genes

steps.description: PCR products were visualized in 1% agarose (Maximus, Lodz, Poland) gel...
steps.searchable_summary: Visualize PCR products using agarose gel electrophoresis
outputs: Gel image showing PCR product bands

steps.description: ...and then purified using Exonuclease I & Polar-BAP (EURx, Gda nsk, Poland) protocol.
steps.searchable_summary: Purify PCR products

steps.description: PCR products were sent to oligo.pl DNA Sequencing Laboratory IBB PAS, Warsaw, Poland for Sanger sequencing with use of BigDye Terminator v. 3.1 chemistry and ABI3730 xl sequencer.
steps.searchable_summary: Send purified PCR products for Sanger sequencing

steps.description: Regarding the remaining clones in Table 1, the DNA extraction and amplification follows Ashworth (2013).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform DNA extraction and amplification for other clones following a referenced protocol

methods.description: Describes the process of performing Maximum Likelihood (ML) phylogenetic analysis on a combined dataset of SSU, rbcL, and psbC genes. Includes details on outgroup selection, sequence alignment, site filtering, data partitioning, evolutionary model selection, tree inference, and bootstrap analysis.

steps.description: ML analysis was performed with a three-gene (SSU, rbcL, and psbC) data set (Appendix S1 in the Supporting Information)...
steps.searchable_summary: Combine SSU, rbcL, and psbC gene sequences into a single dataset

steps.description: ...using two strains of Bolidomonas pacifica L. Guillou & M.J. Chr etiennot-Dinet as the outgroups.
steps.searchable_summary: Include outgroup sequences in the dataset

steps.description: The secondary structural alignment of SSU primary sequences was aligned by SSU-align (Nawrocki 2009) using covariance models.
steps.searchable_summary: Align SSU sequences using SSU-align and covariance models

steps.description: The ambiguous sites with a posterior probability less than the default of 0.9 were removed.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove ambiguous sites from SSU alignment based on posterior probability
inputs: Aligned SSU sequences with posterior probabilities

steps.description: In the ML analysis, the data set was partitioned by different genes, different codon positions (in case of chloroplast markers), and paired and unpaired sites (in case of SSU markers)...
steps.searchable_summary: Partition the concatenated dataset for phylogenetic analysis

steps.description: ...with a GTR+G+I model.
steps.searchable_summary: Select GTR+G+I evolutionary model for phylogenetic analysis

steps.description: Phylogenetic tree was conducted with 1,000 bootstrap replicates using rapid Bootstrap analysis in RAxML v8.1 (Stamatakis 2014).
steps.searchable_summary: Infer Maximum Likelihood phylogenetic tree using RAxML with rapid bootstrap

steps.description: Phylogenetic tree was conducted with 1,000 bootstrap replicates using rapid Bootstrap analysis in RAxML v8.1 (Stamatakis 2014).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform 1000 bootstrap replicates for tree support
outputs: Bootstrap support values for tree branches

steps.description: The best-scoring ML tree was chosen as the final tree...
steps.searchable_summary: Select the best-scoring Maximum Likelihood tree
inputs: Set of ML trees from RAxML run

steps.description: ...and bootstrap values were added to the corresponding nodes.
steps.searchable_summary: Add bootstrap support values to the final tree nodes
outputs: Final phylogenetic tree with bootstrap values
